detail.rejected_7: all 7 reproduce flagship's candle values (eff6 within 0.004, cnt12 exact); all genuinely fail (4 on eff6<0.10, 3 on cnt12=7)
detail.pass_33_vs_candle: eff6 feed-vs-candle jitter up to 0.22; cnt12 off by +/-1 in 13/40; 4 pass-records the candle calls FAIL (all cnt12 6-vs-7 borderline, one also eff6 0.0898, all in the Jul-13 01:00-02:00 cascade cluster)
detail.direction: every disagreement runs bot-LOOSER-than-candle; candle-recomputed retention therefore UNDERSTATES the bot's effective retention -- the convention mismatch cannot move retention back inside [0.40,0.70]
detail.agreement: 36/40 gate decisions agree; 100% agreement outside the borderline cnt12=6/7 cascade cluster

D_test_increment.note: fixed 0.51 fill; ties excluded; a3 claimed +4.69c p=0.0625 on its trigger set

deploy_spec_numbers.note: deploy_spec.json's retention lines describe the walk-forward/TRAIN-calibrated (A=0.32) gate; FINAL-DESIGN's 'expected 0.5-0.6' and Phase-0 band [0.40,0.70] match THOSE numbers, not the deployed A=0.10 gate

C_band_anchor.conclusion: the deployed A=0.10/B=6 gate was ALREADY at retention 0.714 on TEST (Jun 26-Jul 13) before launch under the bot's own convention -- above the band's 0.70 cap on day 0. Live 0.758 (n=62, z=0.78 vs TEST) and last-21d 0.717 are statistically indistinguishable from pre-launch TEST. The out-of-band reading is a band mis-registration (anchored on the A=0.32 arm), not a regime shift and not a gate-code bug.

multiplicity_accounting.live_increment: single pre-registered comparison (day-60 gate-verdict analog); result is a null -- no selection correction can rescue or harm it; CI90 [-35.7,+30.3]c means it is uninformative, not 'negative'
multiplicity_accounting.TEST_increment: reproduced +4.76c, 1h-block-boot one-sided p=0.051 (a3: +4.69c p=0.0625). Additionally OPTIMISTICALLY biased: A=0.10/B=6 was calibrated on trailing-20d asof Jul-10 (Jun-20..Jul-10), overlapping TEST (Jun-26..Jul-13) by ~15d. Honest read is weaker than p=0.06 -> increment unproven, as claimed
multiplicity_accounting.retention_conventions_searched_here: K=5 conventions x 2 params x 4 windows = 40 cells searched ADVERSARIALLY to find any convention inside the band; only trigger-INCLUSIVE cnt12 (not the bot's verified convention, contradicted by 40/40 logged-decision checks) reaches 0.675-0.678 -- barely inside; every convention consistent with the bot's logs stays outside
multiplicity_accounting.train_test_hygiene: R7's null claims cannot benefit from selection; the one significant-looking number it relies on (candle LEVEL +4.55c TEST p=0.0035) was pre-registered in the 07-10 round before Jul-10-13 data existed

confirmed: live increment -2.57c (n=34/7, perm p=0.792) reproduced to the cent
all 7 gate rejections genuine on candles
TEST increment not significant (p~0.05-0.06), and contaminated optimistically by deploy calibration overlap
retention outside [0.40,0.70] under the bot's verified convention (live 0.758, last21d 0.717)
day-60 increment underpowered at ~25-27% rejection mass (~2.8 rejected fills/day -> SE ~4.3c at day 60)
refuted: 'regime shift (fewer cascades)': TEST-wide retention was ALREADY 0.714 pre-launch; last-21d 0.717 is no shift (z~0); live-window z=0.78 vs TEST baseline
implicit 'gate-code bug' reading of the Phase-0 flag: 36/40 logged decisions match candles; the 4 borderline mismatches make the bot LOOSER, worsening not curing the excursion
verdict_summary.corrected_action: log the excursion as a BAND MIS-REGISTRATION (Phase-0 band anchored on the A=0.32 train-calibrated arm's 0.41-0.52 retention; deployed A=0.10 arm was at 0.71 on TEST before launch). Do NOT treat as gate-code bug, do NOT reset the Phase-0 clock, no parameter change. Re-register the band around the deployed arm's design-time value (e.g. 0.60-0.80) and note the feed-vs-candle cnt12 jitter (+/-1 on ~1/3 of borderline signals) for day-60 diagnostics.
